Verified Petition For Orders Suspension And Removal Of Kenneth Lewis As Successor Trustee And Appointment Of Patrick Johnson As Successor Trustee; Requiring Respondent To Provide An Accounting In Accordance With Probate Code 1060 Et Seq.; Compelling The Return Of Property Wrongfully Taken, Recovery Of Compensatory Damages And Double Damages Pursuant To Probate Code 850-859; Requiring Respondent To Hold Settlor'S Property In A Constructive Trust For The
Benefit Of The Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Continued To Jun-30-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Continued To Jun-30-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Continued To Jun-30-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
Off Calendar Dec-02-2025 Continued To Apr-29-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Scott Jordan, for Lois F. Flowers, Petitioner.
Endy Ukoha-Ajike, for Ken Lewis, Trustee.

At the outset, the court confirms Mr. Ukoha-Ajike's intent to file a reply to Mr. Jordan's opposition to Ken Lewis' accounting petition. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the status of the subject matters. At the court's query, and after discussion regarding possible settlement efforts, the matter is referred to Pro Bono mediation and the order referring the matter to Mediation is executed in open court. Pursuant to the order, mediation must be completed no later than Apr-15-2026. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Apr-22-2026. The subject matters are continued to Apr-29-2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and a status of mediation shall be added for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Ramirez. Not Reported.

Petition For Cancellation Of Deed Under Civil Code 3412, For Relief Under Probate Code 850 Et Seq., And For Damages Under Probate Code 859
Off Calendar Jan-27-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Endy Ukoha-Ajike for Kenneth Lewis, Petition (via Zoom).
Scott Jordan for Michael Freeman, etc., Beneficiaries (via Zoom).

Discussion was held with regards to Ralph Hall, Jr. Counsel indicated that Ralph Hall, Jr. has been served but has not engaged in communication or appeared in court. No further information is available regarding his whereabouts or willingness to participate. Counsel indicated that mediation has not occurred and is unlikely given the lack of communication from Mr. Hall. Counsel requested the court proceed with resolving the matter without mediation.

Counsel noted that Ralph Hall, Jr. filed documents attempting to transfer property in violation of a prior court order. Title company exhibits B and C attached to the declaration indicate the company is unable to determine the intent of the documents and will not issue title.

The court took the petition under submission and stated it will issue an order.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
